observations.title: CodexSdk interface needs constructor signature update for env option
observations.rationale: CodexSdk declares `Codex: new () => CodexInstance` with no arguments. Requirement 1 passes `{ env: ... }` to the constructor, so the type needs expanding to `new (options?: { env?: Record<string, string>; apiKey?: string }) => CodexInstance`. External constraint is documented in the request.

observations.title: TC-VL-08 expectation encodes buggy maskSensitive behaviour
observations.rationale: TC-VL-08 expects `sk-ant-api03_...` — the current (buggy) output that exposes the body before the first underscore. After the requirement-5 fix the correct output would be `sk-ant-...`. This test must be updated alongside the fix.

observations.title: B-6 scope expansion may surface existing benign process.env references in adapter/ and util/
observations.rationale: After expanding the B-6 grep to src/adapter/ and src/util/, at least two pre-existing benign process.env references will be flagged: getDebugSubsystems() in src/util/env-filter.ts (reads SPECRUNNER_DEBUG) and the OAuth token resolver call in src/adapter/claude-code/agent-runner.ts:271. Both must be added to arch-allowlist.ts. Request already states 'existing seam-via call-sites (e.g. claude adapter) shall be allowed'.

findings.title: T-11 allowlist pattern for claude-code/agent-runner.ts does not match the grep-flagged line
findings.rationale: The primary pattern `resolveClaudeCodeOAuthTokenFn` is specified for the allowlist entry covering `src/adapter/claude-code/agent-runner.ts`. The actual grep-flagged line is line 271 (`process.env as Record<string, string | undefined>,`), which does not contain the function name. Using the primary pattern verbatim would leave an unallowlisted violation and cause the B-6 test to fail. The spec's own Note already identifies this ambiguity and provides the correct alternative pattern `"as Record<string, string | undefined>"`. The implementer must apply the Note's option (b).

observations.title: All six vulnerable call-sites verified against actual source
observations.rationale: codex runner (no env option), git-exec runSubprocess (no env), verification git show (no env), SECRET_DENYLIST (5 fixed keys), MASK_PATTERNS (no i flag / indexOf underscore bug), B-6 grep scope (src/core/ only) — all confirmed in the worktree.

observations.title: filterViolations applies isCommentLine — JSDoc mentions in env-filter.ts are auto-excluded
observations.rationale: The test harness's filterViolations already calls isCommentLine(), so the three JSDoc comment lines in env-filter.ts that mention process.env (lines 4, 22, 32) are filtered before the allowlist check. Only line 25 (the real non-comment read) requires an allowlist entry, which the spec correctly provides.

message: spec-review iteration 1 completed with verdict: approved
